steps.title: Identify the turning point
steps.content: The key signal is “Recently, however” in the third sentence. This phrase marks a shift between what earlier Native writers (1960s-1970s) were doing and what younger Native writers are doing now.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A works because the passage follows exactly this pattern: it establishes earlier Native fiction (Silko and peers: realistic, serious) and then describes how recent Native fiction differs (younger writers: popular, entertaining genres). The other three either misread the passage as judgment between writers or introduce comparisons not present in the text.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
